title: Multi-Agent Delegation, Context Minimization, Merge, and Failure Containment · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds typed contained delegation graph from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of typed contained delegation graph while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
Define delegated goal, inputs, outputs, non-goals, authority, tools, budget, deadline, evidence, stop, and escalation in a typed contract.
Send only task-required, tenant-authorized, provenance-labeled context and never inherit parent secrets or tools implicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Delegation Fit
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 1409 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to delegate bounded tasks with typed contracts, minimum context, independent authority, result validation, conflict resolution, and failure containment; every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy; then change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Delegation Failure Containment
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-delegation-failure-containment-1pkpov" that uses case 1050 to delegate bounded tasks with typed contracts, minimum context, independent authority, result validation, conflict resolution, and failure containment. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Delegation Failure Containment.

For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Delegation Failure Containment, keep Delegation Failure Containment connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-delegation-failure-containment-1pkpov
# Competency: agentpy-delegation-failure-containment.
# Case 1050: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def evidence_agentpy_delegation_failure_containment_1pkpov(delegated_tasks: int = 18, accepted_results: int = 12, rejected_results: int = 6, minimized_contexts: int = 18, cascading_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pkpov = "1pkpov-vopkp1-1050"
    assert evidence_variant_1pkpov
    evidence_axes_1pkpov = (
        "authority1pkpov",
        "interaction1050",
        "toolvopkp1",
        "state1pkpov1050",
        "evaluation10501pkpov",
        "recoveryvopkp11050",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pkpov)) == 6
    if delegated_tasks < 6 or accepted_results < 0 or rejected_results < 0:
        return False, delegated_tasks, "delegation-count"
    if accepted_results + rejected_results != delegated_tasks:
        return False, accepted_results, "delegation-reconciliation"
    if minimized_contexts != delegated_tasks:
        return False, minimized_contexts, "delegation-context"
    if cascading_failures > 0:
        return False, cascading_failures, "delegation-cascade"
    assert accepted_results + rejected_results == delegated_tasks and cascading_failures == 0
    return True, accepted_results, "contained-agent-delegation"
